name: Encode a JavaScript object into protobuf bytes
info:
The encode node verifies `msg.payload` against the configured protobuf message type (`TestType`) and outputs the binary wire bytes as a Buffer.

Click the inject button and open the debug sidebar: `msg.payload` is now a Buffer and `msg.protobufType` names the message type that was used.

Tip: setting `msg.protobufType` on an incoming message overrides the type configured in the node.

The protobuf-file config node points at:

    node_modules/node-red-contrib-protobuf/examples/protos/proto3.proto

This relative path works when Node-RED runs from your user directory (for example `~/.node-red`). If the proto file fails to load, open the protobuf-file config node and enter the absolute path on your machine.
